name: ControlPoint
summary: Represents control point geometry with three-dimensional position and weight.

constructors.name: ControlPoint(Point3d)
constructors.summary: Constructs a new world 3-D, or Euclidean, control point. The 4-D representation of this is (x, y, z, 1.0).

constructors.name: ControlPoint(Point4d)
constructors.summary: Constructs a new homogeneous control point, where the 4-D representation is (x, y, z, w). The world 3-D, or Euclidean, representation is (x/w, y/w, z/w).
constructors.remarks: For expert use only. If you do not understand homogeneous coordinates, then use an override that accepts world 3-D, or Euclidean, coordinates as input.

constructors.name: ControlPoint(Point3d, Double)
constructors.summary: Constructs a control point from a world 3-D, or Euclidean, location and a weight. The world 3-D, or Euclidean, representation is (x/w, y/w, z/w).

params.name: weight
params.doc: Weight factor of the control point. You should not use weights less than or equal to zero.

constructors.name: ControlPoint(Double, Double, Double)
constructors.signature: public ControlPoint(double x, double y, double z)
constructors.summary: Constructs a new world 3-D, or Euclidean, control point. The 4-D representation is (x, y, z, 1.0).

params.name: x
params.doc: X coordinate of the control point.

params.name: y
params.doc: Y coordinate of the control point.

params.name: z
params.doc: Z coordinate of the control point.

constructors.name: ControlPoint(Double, Double, Double, Double)
constructors.signature: public ControlPoint(double x, double y, double z, double weight)
constructors.summary: Constructs a new homogeneous control point, where the 4-D representation is (x, y, z, w). The world 3-D, or Euclidean, representation is (x/w, y/w, z/w).
constructors.remarks: For expert use only. If you do not understand homogeneous coordinates, then use an override that accepts world 3-D, or Euclidean, coordinates as input.

params.name: x
params.doc: X coordinate of the control point.

params.name: y
params.doc: Y coordinate of the control point.

params.name: z
params.doc: Z coordinate of the control point.

params.name: weight
params.doc: Weight factor of the control point. You should not use weights less than or equal to zero.

methods.name: EpsilonEquals
methods.signature: public bool EpsilonEquals(ControlPoint other, double epsilon)
methods.summary: Check that all values in other are within epsilon of the values in this

methods.name: Equals(ControlPoint)
methods.summary: Determines if two points exactly match.

returns.doc: True if the other control point exactly matches this one.

properties.name: Location
properties.summary: Gets or sets world 3-D, or Euclidean location of the control point.

properties.name: Unset
properties.summary: Gets the predefined, unset control point.

properties.name: Weight
properties.summary: Gets or sets the weight of this control point.

properties.name: X
properties.summary: Gets or sets the X coordinate of the control point.

properties.name: Y
properties.summary: Gets or sets the Y coordinate of the control point.

properties.name: Z
properties.summary: Gets or sets the Z coordinate of the control point.
